source.sourcePath: raw/sources/Project/02_CYAN/1. DHF/26. 사용적합성 엔지니어링 보고서/1. A-UEP-CYN_Usability Validation Plan (Rev.0 250525).docx

document.title: Usability Validation Plan for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: FDA Guidance - Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models GT1717C and GT1717CP.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Outlines usability validation activities to ensure the device meets usability requirements, provides a safe and effective user experience, and complies with applicable standards and regulations.
keyPoints: Applies to usability validation activities for GT1717C and GT1717CP, focusing on safety-critical tasks, user interface evaluation, and error minimization.
Lists applicable standards and guidance including IEC 60601-1-6, IEC 62366-1, FDA human factors guidance, and ISO 14971.
Defines representative user groups as radiologists, technicians, and clinicians in hospital settings, mobile clinics, and emergency response units.
Summative evaluation is planned to test the final design in real-world conditions with representative users.

evidence.claim: The document is a usability validation plan for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models GT1717C and GT1717CP.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: GT1717C, GT1717CP

The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on May 25, 2025.

Rev. No. 0 Rev. date May 25, 2025 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The plan applies to usability validation activities for GT1717C and GT1717CP and focuses on safety-critical tasks, user interface evaluation, and error minimization.
evidence.evidenceText: This plan applies to all usability validation activities for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector GT1717C, GT1717CP, focusing on safety-critical tasks, user interface evaluation, and error minimization.

evidence.claim: Applicable standards and guidance include IEC 60601-1-6:2010/AMD2:2020, IEC 62366-1:2015, FDA human factors guidance, and ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicable Standards and Guidelines IEC 60601-1-6 :2010/AMD2:2020 ... IEC 62366-1:2015 ... FDA Guidance - Applying Human Factors and Usability Engineering to Medical Devices ISO 14971

evidence.claim: GT1717C and GT1717CP are wired digital flat panel detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP are wired digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The expected service life is 5 years.
evidence.pageTarget: GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The two model variants differ only in CsI deposition thickness and are identical in usability.
evidence.evidenceText: The two products differ only in CsI deposition thickness, as shown below, and are identical in usability. GT1717C : 300um GT1717CP : 500um

evidence.claim: Validation objectives are to verify safe and effective use by intended users in intended use environments and to identify and mitigate residual usability risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Verify that the product can be used safely and effectively by intended users in the intended use environments. Identify and mitigate any residual usability risks.

evidence.claim: Validation user groups include radiologists, technicians, and clinicians.
evidence.evidenceText: User Groups: Radiologists, technicians, and clinicians with varying levels of expertise.

evidence.claim: Validation use environments include hospital settings, mobile clinics, and emergency response units.
evidence.evidenceText: Use Environments: Hospital settings, mobile clinics, emergency response units.

evidence.claim: Success criteria include task completion within expected time and no safety-critical errors during tasks.
evidence.evidenceText: Success Criteria: Task completion within the expected time. No safety-critical errors during tasks.

evidence.claim: Failure criteria include any task failure resulting in potential harm or user frustration exceeding predefined thresholds.
evidence.evidenceText: Failure Criteria: Any task failure resulting in potential harm. User frustration exceeding predefined thresholds (e.g., multiple retries).

evidence.claim: Summative evaluation is planned to use 15 or more participants in diverse clinical scenarios.
evidence.evidenceText: User testing with 15+ participants in diverse clinical scenarios.

evidence.claim: Formative evaluations were completed by approximately May 20, 2025, and summative evaluations were planned from September 01, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Formative evaluations: Completed by [~ May 20, 2025]. Summative evaluations: Planned for [September 01, 2025 ~].

reviewFlags.reason: Manufacturer or legal entity issuing the plan is not identified in the provided source text.
Authority signal is FDA, but the source text only lists FDA guidance as an applicable guideline and does not state a submission authority or jurisdiction.
Source path uses document code A-UEP-CYN while the document text states Report No.: A-UVP-CYN (Rev.0).
Some text includes OCR or transcription artifacts and typos, including 'Succes' and mixed English/Korean duplicated phrases.
Schedule dates use bracketed approximate/open-ended notation for formative and summative evaluations.
